task0.objective: Develop Python code to parse the job description and extract required skills and responsibilities

history.result:
```python
import re

def extract_skills_responsibilities(job_description):
    """
    Extracts skills and responsibilities from a job description.

    Args:
        job_description (str): The job description text.

    Returns:
        dict: A dictionary containing lists of skills and responsibilities.
              Returns None if extraction fails.
    """

    if not isinstance(job_description, str) or not job_description:
        print("Error: Job description must be a non-empty string.")
        return None

    skills = []
    responsibilities = []

    # Skill extraction (using regex, customize as needed)
    skill_patterns = [
        r"(?i)\b(?:proficient in|experience with|knowledge of|familiar with|expertise in|skilled in)\s+(?:[a-zA-Z#]+(?:,\s*[a-zA-Z#]+)*(?:and\s*[a-zA-Z#]+)?)",
        r"(?i)\b[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-zA-Z]+)*(?:\s+skills?)\b", # e.g., Python skills, Java skills
        r"(?i)\b(?:skills:)\s*(.+)",  # skills: python, java
        r"(?i)\b(?:skillset:)\s*(.+)" # skillset: python, java
    ]

    for pattern in skill_patterns:
        matches = re.findall(pattern, job_description)
        for match in matches:
            # Clean up matches (remove prefixes, split into individual skills)
            cleaned_match = re.sub(r"(?i)(?:proficient in|experience with|knowledge of|familiar with|expertise in|skilled in|skills:|skillset:)\s*", "", match).strip()
            individual_skills = re.split(r",\s*|and\s*", cleaned_match)
            skills.extend([skill.strip() for skill in individual_skills if skill.strip()])

    # Responsibility extraction (using sentence splitting and keyword identification)
    responsibility_keywords = ["responsible for", "responsibilities include", "duties include", "manage", "develop", "support", "lead", "coordinate", "implement", "maintain"]
    sentences = re.split(r"(?<!\w\.\w.)(?<![A-Z][a-z]\.)(?<=\.|\?)\s", job_description) # Split into sentences
    for sentence in sentences:
        for keyword in responsibility_keywords:
            if keyword in sentence.lower():
                responsibilities.append(sentence.strip())
                break  # Avoid adding the same sentence multiple times


    # Further cleaning and deduplication
    skills = list(set(skills)) # Remove duplicates
    responsibilities = list(set(responsibilities)) # Remove duplicates

    return {"skills": skills, "responsibilities": responsibilities}

if __name__ == '__main__':
    job_description = """
    Job Title: Software Engineer

    Responsibilities include designing, developing, and testing software applications.
    Responsible for maintaining existing systems and implementing new features.
    Duties include collaborating with cross-functional teams.

    Proficient in Python, Java, and C++. Experience with cloud technologies like AWS and Azure.
    Knowledge of database systems such as MySQL and PostgreSQL.
    Python skills are required.
    Strong analytical skills.
    skills: javascript, HTML, CSS.
    skillset: Docker, Kubernetes
    """

    extracted_data = extract_skills_responsibilities(job_description)

    if extracted_data:
        print("Skills:", extracted_data["skills"])
        print("Responsibilities:", extracted_data["responsibilities"])
    else:
        print("Extraction failed.")
```

task1.objective: Develop Python code to parse the candidate resume and extract skills and experience

history.result:
```python
import re

def extract_skills_and_experience(resume_text):
    """
    Extracts skills and experience from a resume text.

    Args:
        resume_text: The text content of the resume.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing lists of skills and experience entries.
    """

    skills = extract_skills(resume_text)
    experience = extract_experience(resume_text)

    return {
        "skills": skills,
        "experience": experience
    }

def extract_skills(resume_text):
    """
    Extracts skills from the resume text using regex.

    Args:
        resume_text: The text content of the resume.

    Returns:
        A list of skills.
    """

    # Define common skill keywords and patterns
    skill_patterns = [
        r"(Python|Java|C++|JavaScript|SQL|HTML|CSS|React|Angular|Node\.js)",
        r"(Data Science|Machine Learning|Deep Learning|Natural Language Processing)",
        r"(Cloud Computing|AWS|Azure|GCP)",
        r"(Project Management|Agile|Scrum)",
        r"(Communication|Leadership|Teamwork)"
    ]

    skills = []
    for pattern in skill_patterns:
        matches = re.findall(pattern, resume_text, re.IGNORECASE)
        skills.extend(matches)
    
    # Remove duplicates while preserving order
    unique_skills = []
    [unique_skills.append(x) for x in skills if x not in unique_skills]

    return unique_skills

def extract_experience(resume_text):
    """
    Extracts experience entries from the resume text using regex.

    Args:
        resume_text: The text content of the resume.

    Returns:
        A list of experience entries.
    """
    # Define pattern for experience sections
    experience_pattern = r"(?i)(?:Experience|Employment History)([\s\S]*?)(?:Education|Skills|References|$)"

    match = re.search(experience_pattern, resume_text)
    if match:
      experience_section = match.group(1)

      #Split experience section into individual entries based on common separators like dates or job titles
      experience_entries = re.split(r"\d{4}[-–—]\d{4}|\d{4}\s*-\s*(Present|Current)|\b(?:[A-Z][a-z]+\s)+[A-Z][a-z]+(?:,\s(?:[A-Z][a-z]+))?\b", experience_section)
      
      #Clean up the entries by removing whitespace and empty strings
      experience_entries = [entry.strip() for entry in experience_entries if entry.strip()]

      return experience_entries

    return []



if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example usage:
    resume_text = """
    John Doe
    john.doe@example.com
    (123) 456-7890

    Summary
    A highly motivated and experienced software engineer with a passion for developing innovative solutions.

    Experience
    Software Engineer, Acme Corp, 2018-2022
    - Developed and maintained web applications using Python, JavaScript, and React.
    - Designed and implemented REST APIs using Node.js.
    - Worked with databases such as SQL and MongoDB.

    Senior Software Engineer, Beta Inc, 2022-Present
    - Led a team of engineers in the development of a new mobile application.
    - Utilized cloud computing platforms such as AWS and Azure.
    - Implemented machine learning algorithms for data analysis.

    Education
    Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, University of Example, 2018

    Skills
    Python, Java, JavaScript, SQL, HTML, CSS, React, Node.js, Data Science, Machine Learning, Cloud Computing, AWS, Azure, Project Management, Agile, Scrum, Communication, Leadership, Teamwork

    References
    Available upon request.
    """

    extracted_data = extract_skills_and_experience(resume_text)
    print("Extracted Skills:", extracted_data['skills'])
    print("Extracted Experience:", extracted_data['experience'])
```

task2.objective: Implement Python code to match extracted resume data against job requirements and build a candidate profile

history.result:
```python
def create_candidate_profile(job_description, resume_data):
    """
    Matches extracted resume data against job requirements and builds a candidate profile.

    Args:
        job_description: A string containing the job description.
        resume_data: A dictionary containing extracted resume data (skills and experience).

    Returns:
        A dictionary representing the candidate profile, including:
            - skills_match: A list of skills from the resume that match the job description.
            - experience_match: A list of experience entries from the resume that align with the job description.
            - overall_fit: A score (0-100) indicating the overall fit of the candidate for the job.
    """

    skills_match = find_matching_skills(job_description, resume_data['skills'])
    experience_match = find_matching_experience(job_description, resume_data['experience'])
    overall_fit = calculate_overall_fit(job_description, resume_data)

    candidate_profile = {
        "skills_match": skills_match,
        "experience_match": experience_match,
        "overall_fit": overall_fit
    }

    return candidate_profile

def find_matching_skills(job_description, resume_skills):
    """
    Identifies skills from the resume that match the job description.

    Args:
        job_description: A string containing the job description.
        resume_skills: A list of skills extracted from the resume.

    Returns:
        A list of skills from the resume that are present in the job description.
    """

    matching_skills = [skill for skill in resume_skills if skill.lower() in job_description.lower()]
    return matching_skills

def find_matching_experience(job_description, resume_experience):
    """
    Identifies experience entries from the resume that align with the job description.

    Args:
        job_description: A string containing the job description.
        resume_experience: A list of experience entries extracted from the resume.

    Returns:
        A list of experience entries from the resume that are relevant to the job description.
    """

    matching_experience = [exp for exp in resume_experience if exp.lower() in job_description.lower()]
    return matching_experience

def calculate_overall_fit(job_description, resume_data):
    """
    Calculates an overall fit score for the candidate based on skills and experience.

    Args:
        job_description: A string containing the job description.
        resume_data: A dictionary containing extracted resume data (skills and experience).

    Returns:
        An integer representing the overall fit score (0-100).
    """

    # Placeholder logic for calculating overall fit.  This can be refined with more sophisticated methods.
    skills_match = find_matching_skills(job_description, resume_data['skills'])
    experience_match = find_matching_experience(job_description, resume_data['experience'])

    skill_points = len(skills_match) * 15  #Weight each skill match as 15 points
    experience_points = len(experience_match) * 20 #Weight each experience match as 20 points

    overall_fit = skill_points + experience_points

    # Cap the score at 100
    overall_fit = min(overall_fit, 100)

    return overall_fit

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example usage:
    job_description = """
    We are looking for a skilled Software Engineer with experience in Python, JavaScript, and React.
    The ideal candidate should have experience with cloud computing platforms such as AWS and Azure.
    Experience with data science and machine learning is a plus.
    """

    resume_data = {
        "skills": ["Python", "JavaScript", "React", "AWS", "Azure", "Data Science", "SQL"],
        "experience": [
            "Developed web applications using Python, JavaScript, and React.",
            "Worked with cloud computing platforms such as AWS and Azure.",
            "Implemented machine learning algorithms for data analysis."
        ]
    }

    candidate_profile = create_candidate_profile(job_description, resume_data)
    print("Candidate Profile:", candidate_profile)
```

task3.objective: Create the interview engine in Python that generates and delivers technical questions based on job requirements and candidate profile

history.result:
```python
import random

class InterviewEngine:
    def __init__(self, job_description, resume):
        """
        Initializes the InterviewEngine with the job description and candidate resume.
        Args:
            job_description (dict): A dictionary containing job description details like skills and responsibilities.
            resume (dict): A dictionary containing candidate resume details like skills and experience.
        """
        self.job_description = job_description
        self.resume = resume
        self.question_bank = {
            "Python": [
                "Explain the difference between lists and tuples in Python.",
                "What are decorators in Python?",
                "How does garbage collection work in Python?",
            ],
            "Java": [
                "Explain the difference between == and .equals() in Java.",
                "What are the principles of Object-Oriented Programming?",
                "How does the JVM work?",
            ],
            "JavaScript": [
                "What is the difference between '==' and '===' in JavaScript?",
                "Explain closures in JavaScript.",
                "What are promises in JavaScript?",
            ],
            "SQL": [
                "Explain the difference between INNER JOIN and OUTER JOIN.",
                "What is a primary key?",
                "How can you optimize SQL queries?",
            ],
            "Data Science": [
                "Explain the difference between supervised and unsupervised learning.",
                "What is the bias-variance tradeoff?",
                "How do you handle missing data?",
            ],
            "Machine Learning": [
                "Explain the difference between different machine learning algorithms?",
                "What is regularization?",
                "How do you evaluate the performance of a machine learning model?",
            ],
            "Cloud Computing": [
                "Explain the difference between IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS.",
                "What are the benefits of using cloud computing?",
                "How does cloud autoscaling work?",
            ],
            "AWS": [
                "What is EC2?",
                "What is S3?",
                "How do you deploy an application on AWS?",
            ],
            "Azure": [
                "What is Azure Virtual Machines?",
                "What is Azure Blob Storage?",
                "How do you deploy an application on Azure?",
            ],
            "Project Management": [
                "What are the different phases of project management?",
                "Explain Agile methodologies.",
                "How do you manage project risks?",
            ]
        }

    def generate_questions(self, num_questions=5):
        """
        Generates technical questions based on the job description and candidate resume.
        Args:
            num_questions (int): The number of questions to generate.
        Returns:
            list: A list of technical questions.
        """
        relevant_skills = set(self.job_description['skills']).intersection(set(self.resume['skills']))
        questions = []
        for skill in relevant_skills:
            if skill in self.question_bank:
                questions.extend(self.question_bank[skill])

        selected_questions = random.sample(questions, min(num_questions, len(questions))) # Avoid exceeding list length
        return selected_questions

    def deliver_question(self, question):
        """
        Delivers a technical question to the candidate (simulated).
        Args:
            question (str): The technical question to deliver.
        Returns:
            None
        """
        print(f"Question: {question}")

    def get_candidate_response(self):
        """
        Simulates getting a response from the candidate.
        Returns:
            str: The candidate's response.
        """
        return input("Your Answer: ")

if __name__ == '__main__':
    # Example Usage
    job_description = {
        "skills": ["Python", "Data Science", "Machine Learning"],
        "responsibilities": ["Develop machine learning models", "Analyze data"]
    }
    resume = {
        "skills": ["Python", "Data Science", "Machine Learning", "SQL"],
        "experience": ["Developed machine learning models using Python"]
    }

    interview_engine = InterviewEngine(job_description, resume)
    questions = interview_engine.generate_questions(num_questions=3)

    for question in questions:
        interview_engine.deliver_question(question)
        candidate_response = interview_engine.get_candidate_response()
        print(f"Candidate's Response: {candidate_response}\n")
```

Call connect() first.") return False message = MIMEMultipart("alternative") message["Subject"] = subject message["From"] = sender_email message["To"] = recipient_email if is_html: part = MIMEText(body, "html") else: part = MIMEText(body, "plain") message.attach(part) try: self.server.sendmail(sender_email, recipient_email, message.as_string()) print(f"Email sent successfully to {recipient_email}") return True except Exception as e: print(f"Failed to send email: {e}") return False def send_invitation(self, sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link): """Sends an interview invitation email.""" subject = "Interview Invitation" body = f""" Dear Candidate, You are invited to an interview on {interview_time}. Please join the interview using the following link: {interview_link} Best regards, The Hiring Team """ return self.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, subject, body) def send_reminder(self, sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link): """Sends an interview reminder email.""" subject = "Interview Reminder" body = f""" Dear Candidate, This is a reminder for your interview scheduled on {interview_time}. Please join the interview using the following link: {interview_link} Best regards, The Hiring Team """ return self.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, subject, body) def send_notification(self, sender_email, recipient_email, message_body): """Sends a general notification email.""" subject = "Notification" return self.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, subject, message_body) if __name__ == '__main__': # Example Usage (replace with your actual credentials) smtp_server = "your_smtp_server" # e.g., "smtp.gmail.com" smtp_port = 587 # Or 465 for SSL smtp_username = "your_email@example.com" smtp_password = "your_password" # Consider using environment variables for security email_service = EmailService(smtp_server, smtp_port, smtp_username, smtp_password) # Connect to the SMTP server if email_service.connect(): sender_email = "your_email@example.com" recipient_email = "candidate@example.com" # Send Invitation interview_time = "2024-01-20 10:00 AM" interview_link = "https://meet.google.com/xyz" email_service.send_invitation(sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link) # Send Reminder email_service.send_reminder(sender_email, recipient_email, interview_time, interview_link) # Send Notification notification_message = "Your application has been received." email_service.send_notification(sender_email, recipient_email, notification_message) # Send HTML email html_body = """

""" email_service.send_email(sender_email, recipient_email, "Application Received", html_body, is_html=True) # Disconnect from the SMTP server email_service.disconnect() ```
